# Cuebook Skills

> Official Cuebook Agent Skills, Codex plugin, Claude Code plugin, and remote MCP distribution for source-linked market intelligence and creator-authored market content.

## Public entrypoints

- [Query Cuebook](plugins/cuebook/skills/query-cuebook/SKILL.md): read-only search, inspection, comparison, evidence, market data, and history. It never writes or invokes Create.
- [Create Cuebook Content](plugins/cuebook/skills/create-cuebook-content/SKILL.md): turns a creator's seed into sourced text, static visuals, and optional settlement artifacts. It may invoke Query for evidence.
- [Author Cuebook Skill](plugins/cuebook/skills/author-cuebook-skill/SKILL.md): packages a creator-authored skill, runs the structural pre-check, confirms one manifest card, and submits it to the community marketplace for review.

Only these three skills are public entrypoints. Supporting capability modules are loaded through them on demand.

## Architecture and distribution

- [Plugin overview](plugins/cuebook/README.md): module boundary, authorization model, installation, and platform support.
- [Platform matrix](plugins/cuebook/platforms/README.md): host-specific Skill, MCP, OAuth, and verification boundaries.
- [Canonical plugin index](plugins/cuebook/assets/plugin-index-v1.json): public entrypoints, catalog version, and package inventory.
- [Module map](plugins/cuebook/assets/cuebook-modules-v1.json): the one-way `Create -> Query` dependency and skill ownership.
- [MCP capability map](plugins/cuebook/assets/mcp-capability-map-v1.json): Cuebook MCP URL, read/write ownership, OAuth scopes, and release gates.
- [Built generic bundles](skills/): self-contained `query-cuebook`, `create-cuebook-content`, and `author-cuebook-skill` packages. These are generated artifacts; do not edit them directly.

The source plugin is under `plugins/cuebook/`. Codex and Claude Code use native plugin marketplaces. Cursor, Hermes, OpenClaw, and generic Agent Skills clients install the three self-contained bundles under `skills/`. Claude, Claude Desktop, ChatGPT, Grok, and generic MCP clients connect directly to the remote endpoint without loading the Skill layer. Credentials never belong in this repository or generated artifacts.

## Deterministic checks

- `npm run validate` checks package/module policy, local resource closure, `$skill-name` closure, and retired capability references.
- `npm test` runs the Node.js test suite.
- `npm run build:release` regenerates the public bundles and `skills/release-manifest.json`.
- `npm run check` runs validation, tests, regeneration, and verifies that the tracked bundles match source.

Node.js 22 or newer is the supported validation runtime. Playwright plus Chromium is required for browser-backed render and audit tests.
